text: I'm Shawn from BBC learning English and
today we're going to look at the
difference between lay and lie so lay
always has an object and it means put
something or someone down carefully
normally in a flat position when I eat I
lay a cloth on the table you can lay a
baby in a cot the past tense is laid but
careful with the spelling I laid all my
cards on the table the verb lie doesn't
have an object and it means that you are
in a flat position or you put yourself
in a flat position so you move on your
own tonight I want to lie on the sofa
and watch a film but be careful now the
past of lie is lay yesterday I lay on
the beach and read my book thank you for
watching that video from BBC learning
english if you liked it why don't you
subscribe and you'll see every video we
release if you want to see more of our
videos just click on the links over here
there's ones there and there will be
fantastic
